political.s: FEC: $32K PAC donations; 70% to Republican, 30% to Democratic committees.  [CA CalAccess + NY COELIG + TX Ethics adds state-level totals.]

charity.s: $220M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.23% of revenue) via Bank of America Charitable Foundation. JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #5 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. Newsweek Most Trustworthy Companies in America — ranked #17 (2024). Operates the Bank Of America Charitable Foundation Inc: $225.6M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.focus: Economic mobility, affordable housing, workforce development, racial justice

environment.s: Banking on Climate Chaos 2024: $333.2B in fossil-fuel financing 2016-2023 (#4 globally).

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Disability:IN Equality Index score 100/100. JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #5 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. Held by 1 ESG/SRI fund(s) (gender-equality-funds) — investor-screen positive signal. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

privacy.s: CFPB consumer complaints: 179812 on record (100 in past 12mo); 100% timely response rate.

execPay.s: Federal banking regulator action (CFPB/OCC): $150M total — $100M to customers + $50M civil penalty for double-dipping on overdraft fees, withholding cash rewards, and opening unauthorized accounts.

wiki.parent: Berkshire Hathaway, The Vanguard Group, BlackRock
wiki.industry: financial services, financial sector, International Standard Industrial Classification
wiki.controversies:
===Controversies===

====Consumer credit controversies====
In January 2008, Bank of America began notifying some customers without payment problems that their interest rates were more than doubled, up to 28%. The bank was criticized for raising rates on customers in good standing, and for declining to explain why it had done so. In September 2009, a Bank of America credit card customer, Ann Minch, posted a video on YouTube criticizing the bank for raising her interest rate. After the video went viral, she was contacted by a Bank of America representative who lowered her rate. The story attracted national attention from television and internet commentators. In 2010, the bank was criticized for allegedly seizing three properties that were not under their ownership, apparently due to incorrect addresses on their legal documents.

====State of Arizona investigation====
In 2010 the state of Arizona launched an investigation into Bank of America for misleading homeowners who sought to modify their mortgage loans. According to the attorney general of Arizona, the bank "repeatedly has deceived" such mortgagors. In response to the investigation, the bank has given some modifications on the condition that the homeowners remove some information criticizing the bank online.

==== Investment in coal mining ====
On May 6, 2015, Bank of America announced it would reduce its financial exposure to coal companies. The announcement came following pressure from universities and environmental groups. The new policy was announced as part of the bank's decision to continue to reduce credit exposure over time to the coal mining sector.

==== Jeffrey Epstein-related lawsuit ====
On 15 October 2025, a class-action lawsuit against Bank of America alleging its reckless disregard in its financial relationship with Jeffrey Epstein was filed.

In February 2026, a U.S. federal judge ruled that parts of a civil lawsuit against Bank of America could proceed, stating that allegations of the bank’s “reckless disregard” were sufficient to support claims under the Trafficking Victims Protection Act. The lawsuit alleged that Bank of America provided banking services to Jeffrey Epstein despite warning signs, and that the bank knowingly benefited from its relationship with him. While some claims were dismissed, the judge allowed key trafficking-related allegations against the bank to move forward. Bank of America denied wrongdoing.
wiki.extract: The Bank of America Corporation is an American multinational investment bank and financial services holding company headquartered at the Bank of America Corporate Center in Charlotte, North Carolina, with investment banking and auxiliary headquarters in Manhattan. The bank was founded by the merger of NationsBank and Bank of America in 1998. It is the second-largest banking institution in the United States and the second-largest bank in the world by market capitalization, both after JPMorgan Chase.

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking in the office area when she tripped on cables on the floor. She fell and struck her face against the corner of a wall, resulting in a severe laceration to her jaw.

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was closing a safe deposit vault when his index and middle fingers were caught between the latch and the door. The employee sustained a finger injury that required surgery.
